skill.name: vibecoder-guide-legacy
skill.description: Guides non-technical users through natural language software development with simple Japanese prompts and next-step suggestions.
skill.summary: Helps non-technical users ask for coding work and understand what to do next.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed and dismissed as false positives. SKILL.md is a read-only guidance document with no executable shell calls, cryptographic code, obfuscated payload, network access, or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The hit at SKILL.md line 126 is an inline Markdown reference to the cc:WIP marker. It is not shell execution and does not invoke Ruby backticks or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 126 contains a literal inline marker inside documentation. No command syntax, tool permission, or execution instruction is present.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The hits at SKILL.md line 3 occur inside frontmatter description text. No cryptographic API, hash operation, or security-sensitive algorithm is defined there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain metadata describing when the skill should load. I found no evidence of weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Static Entropy Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The entropy warning at SKILL.md line 1 appears related to bilingual Markdown content and YAML frontmatter. The file is readable text, not binary, encrypted, or packed content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The complete file is human-readable Markdown and Japanese text. I found no encoded payload or obfuscated executable content.

content.value_statement: Non-technical users often do not know what to ask during a coding session. This skill suggests simple Japanese phrases and next actions for common project states.
actual_capabilities: Recognizes help and next-step questions in Japanese and English.
Explains how to start a project using simple natural language.
Suggests phrases for plans, active tasks, errors, reviews, and commits.
Uses project state cues such as AGENTS.md, Plans.md, and cc:WIP markers.
Keeps explanations simple for users without technical workflow knowledge.
limitations: This is a legacy skill and is marked as superseded by vibecoder-guide.
It provides guidance text and does not implement application features directly.
It only declares Read tool access and depends on existing project files for context.
Most guidance is written for Japanese-speaking users.

use_cases.title: Start a First Project
use_cases.description: Help a non-technical user describe an app idea and choose the first request to make.

use_cases.title: Continue Existing Work
use_cases.description: Show a user how to continue from a plan, active task, or completed phase.

use_cases.title: Recover From Confusion
use_cases.description: Offer plain-language phrases when the user hits an error or does not know the next step.

prompt_templates.title: Ask What To Do Next
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user needs a simple next action.
prompt_templates.prompt: どうすればいい？今の状態を見て、次に言えばよい一文を教えてください。

prompt_templates.title: Start From An Idea
prompt_templates.scenario: User has an app idea but no technical plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: タスク管理アプリを作りたいです。最初に何を頼めばよいか、簡単な言い方で教えてください。

prompt_templates.title: Resume A Planned Project
prompt_templates.scenario: Project has a plan and the user wants to continue.
prompt_templates.prompt: 計画を見て、今始められる作業を教えてください。技術用語は少なくしてください。

prompt_templates.title: Handle Errors And Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced flow where the user wants recovery and quality checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: 直近のエラーと進捗を確認して、修正、動作確認、レビューのどれを頼むべきか説明してください。

output_examples.output: 今はプロジェクトの状態を確認します。まだ計画がなければ、作りたいものを一文で伝える案内を返します。

output: 計画がある場合は、最初の未完了タスクを始める言い方を提案します。
作業中の場合は、続ける、進捗を見る、問題を説明する選択肢を示します.

output_examples.output: 問題の要約を確認し、直して、説明して、スキップして、などの短い依頼文を提案します。

best_practices: Use this skill when the user asks for help, next steps, or simple usage guidance.
Keep responses short and avoid technical terms unless the user asks for detail.
Prefer the newer vibecoder-guide skill when both versions are available.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for direct implementation tasks from technical users.
Do not present legacy guidance as the newest workflow if a replacement skill exists.
Do not treat suggested phrases as proof that a project file or task exists.

faq.question: Who is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is for non-technical users who want to guide coding work with natural language.

faq.question: Does it write code?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and suggested prompts for asking an assistant to do coding work.

faq.question: Why is it marked legacy?
faq.answer: Its metadata says it is deprecated and superseded by vibecoder-guide.

faq.question: What languages does it support?
faq.answer: The main guidance is Japanese, with some English trigger phrases such as help and what should I do.

faq.question: Can it access files?
faq.answer: The skill declares Read tool access and describes checking project context files.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed static findings were false positives, and no malicious behavior was found.
